narrative.headline: 2022 Ford Edge: the power train leads owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 117 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is the most reported area at 30%, followed by back over prevention at 17% and the electrical system at 12%. No fires and no deaths are reported. The electrical system stands out for injuries (3), while the power train, back over prevention, and forward collision avoidance each have 1 crash.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2022 Ford Edge. The contact stated while driving at slow speeds, the vehicle was jerking and shuddering while in low gear. There were no warning lights illuminated. The vehicle was taken to the local dealer, where it was diagnosed that the transmission needed t
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The biggest complaint area: 35 reports (30%), tied to 1 crash. Owners describe jerking or shuddering at low speeds, so feel for rough shifts on a test drive.

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 20 reports (17%), tied to 1 crash. Reports center on the backup camera failing, so check the rear camera in reverse before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 14 reports (12%), and the only area with injuries here (3). No crashes or fires reported, but the injury count makes it worth a close look.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 10 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners mention knocking noises, so listen for unusual sounds from the engine.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The fewest of the top areas: 7 reports (6%), tied to 1 crash. Reports describe sensor errors disabling driver-assist features, so confirm those systems work.
